title: CT-915
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Character |type=Galactic Republic |image= |name=CT-915 |homeworld=[[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]]{{DB|organization|clonetroopers|clone troopers}} |birth= |death=[[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' places the events of the [[MedStar Duology]] in [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]]. [[Drongar/Legends|Drongar]]''[[MedStar I: Battle Surgeons]]'' |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] {{C|[[Cloning/Legends|clone]]}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |donor=[[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] |height=1.83 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] **[[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Were I a civilian, delivered naturally and not vat-born, I could tell you it's none of your business—sir. But since I'm bound to obey my superior officers, then the answer to your question is that I—personally—was pained by Nine-one-five's death. We're all of the same flesh and design, all equal in basic abilities, but he was my comrade in arms. I knew him all my life. We fought together, ate together, and shared our off-duty times like brothers. I miss him. I expect I'll miss him until I die.|CT-914, to Jos Vondar|}} '''CT-915''' was a [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone trooper]] who fought in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] along with fellow [[Trooper/Legends|trooper]] [[CT-914]]. Raised and trained together on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]], the two [[Troop/Legends|troopers]] shared a close bond, viewing each other as brothers. In [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]], both served during the [[Battle of Drongar]], in which CT-915 was [[Death/Legends|killed]]. His death left CT-914 grief-stricken for the rest of his days, until the latter was also killed shortly thereafter in the same battle. ==Biography== {{Quote|Nine-one-five? He, ah, didn't make it.|Jos Vondar|}} CT-915 was one of the many [[Cloning/Legends|clones]] of the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] that were produced on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]] to serve in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]]. CT-915 was decanted just after fellow clone [[CT-914]]; the two trained together and eventually developed a brother-like bond. When the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] broke out, CT-914 and CT-915 fought side-by-side as [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] for the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. In [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]], they were posted on the planet [[Drongar/Legends|Drongar]], where the Republic and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] [[Battle of Drongar|fought]] for control of the valuable [[Bota/Legends|bota]] [[Plant/Legends|plant]] fields; bota possessed great medicinal qualities. Once again, the two friends were inseparable, as they were assigned to the same [[cohort]]. During one of the engagements on the planet, CT-915 was gravely injured and was brought to the [[Republic Mobile Surgical Unit 7]] to receive help; CT-914 helped [[Transport/Legends|transport]] his comrade there. The [[Doctor/Legends|surgeons]] of the RMSU fought for Nine-one-five's life for an entire day, while CT-914 waited for the results outside the surgery. However, the doctors were unable to save the clone's life, and CT-915 [[Death/Legends|died]]. When CT-914 learned of his best friend's death from Doctor [[Jos Vondar]], the [[Trooper/Legends|trooper]] was stricken with grief, although he tried not to show it. CT-914 mourned for his fallen comrade, until he also met his end on Drongar in a Confederate ambush. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Unfortunate. He was…a good soldier. The loss of someone so well trained is…regrettable."''
''"You knew him?''"
''"He was decanted just after me. We trained together, were posted here together, we were part of the same cohort. He…I thought of him as my brother.|CT-914 and Jos Vondar|}} Like all clone [[Troop/Legends|troopers]], CT-915 stood 1.83 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall. Having known each other for their entire lives, CT-915 and CT-914 trained, fought, and spent their off-[[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] together. Although all clone troopers were, in a sense, brothers, as they were copies of Jango Fett, Nine-one-five and Nine-one-four were especially close. CT-914 thought of Nine-one-five as a good and skilled [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] and was deeply saddened by the loss of his best friend. ==Behind the scenes== CT-915 was first mentioned in ''[[MedStar I: Battle Surgeons]]'', the first installment in the [[MedStar Duology]] that was released in [[2004]] and authored by [[Michael Reaves]] and [[Steve Perry]]. ==Appearances== *''[[MedStar I: Battle Surgeons]]'' {{1stm}} *''[[MedStar II: Jedi Healer]]'' {{Mo}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=CT-915 }} [[Category:Clone troopers]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]]
